사무엘상 1:1.content: Now there was a certain man of Ramathaim Zophim, of the hill country of Ephraim, and his name was Elkanah, the son of Jeroham, the son of Elihu, the son of Tohu, the son of Zuph, an Ephraimite.

사무엘상 1:2.content: He had two wives. The name of one was Hannah, and the name of the other Peninnah. Peninnah had children, but Hannah had no children.

사무엘상 1:3.content: This man went up out of his city from year to year to worship and to sacrifice to Yahweh of Armies in Shiloh. The two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, priests to Yahweh, were there.

사무엘상 1:4.content: When the day came that Elkanah sacrificed, he gave to Peninnah his wife, and to all her sons and her daughters, portions;

사무엘상 1:5.content: but to Hannah he gave a double portion, for he loved Hannah, but Yahweh had shut up her womb.

사무엘상 1:6.content: Her rival provoked her severely, to irritate her, because Yahweh had shut up her womb.

사무엘상 1:7.content: So year by year, when she went up to Yahweh’s house, her rival provoked her. Therefore she wept, and didn’t eat.

사무엘상 1:8.content: Elkanah her husband said to her, “Hannah, why do you weep? Why don’t you eat? Why is your heart grieved? Am I not better to you than ten sons?”

사무엘상 1:9.content: So Hannah rose up after they had finished eating and drinking in Shiloh. Now Eli the priest was sitting on his seat by the doorpost of Yahweh’s temple.

사무엘상 1:10.content: She was in bitterness of soul, and prayed to Yahweh, weeping bitterly.

사무엘상 1:11.content: She vowed a vow, and said, “Yahweh of Armies, if you will indeed look at the affliction of your servant and remember me, and not forget your servant, but will give to your servant a boy, then I will give him to Yahweh all the days of his life, and no razor shall come on his head.”

사무엘상 1:12.content: As she continued praying before Yahweh, Eli saw her mouth.

사무엘상 1:13.content: Now Hannah spoke in her heart. Only her lips moved, but her voice was not heard. Therefore Eli thought she was drunk.

사무엘상 1:14.content: Eli said to her, “How long will you be drunk? Get rid of your wine!”

사무엘상 1:15.content: Hannah answered, “No, my lord, I am a woman of a sorrowful spirit. I have not been drinking wine or strong drink, but I poured out my soul before Yahweh.

사무엘상 1:16.content: Don’t consider your servant a wicked woman; for I have been speaking out of the abundance of my complaint and my provocation.”

사무엘상 1:17.content: Then Eli answered, “Go in peace; and may the God of Israel grant your petition that you have asked of him.”

사무엘상 1:18.content: She said, “Let your servant find favor in your sight.” So the woman went her way and ate; and her facial expression wasn’t sad any more.

사무엘상 1:19.content: They rose up in the morning early and worshiped Yahweh, then returned and came to their house to Ramah. Then Elkanah knew Hannah his wife; and Yahweh remembered her.

사무엘상 1:20.content: When the time had come, Hannah conceived, and bore a son; and she named him Samuel, saying, “Because I have asked him of Yahweh.”

사무엘상 1:21.content: The man Elkanah, and all his house, went up to offer to Yahweh the yearly sacrifice and his vow.

사무엘상 1:22.content: But Hannah didn’t go up, for she said to her husband, “Not until the child is weaned; then I will bring him, that he may appear before Yahweh, and stay there forever.”

사무엘상 1:23.content: Elkanah her husband said to her, “Do what seems good to you. Wait until you have weaned him; only may Yahweh establish his word.” So the woman waited and nursed her son until she weaned him.

사무엘상 1:24.content: When she had weaned him, she took him up with her, with three bulls, and one ephah of meal, and a container of wine, and brought him to Yahweh’s house in Shiloh. The child was young.

사무엘상 1:25.content: They killed the bull, and brought the child to Eli.

사무엘상 1:26.content: She said, “Oh, my lord, as your soul lives, my lord, I am the woman who stood by you here, praying to Yahweh.

사무엘상 1:27.content: I prayed for this child, and Yahweh has given me my petition which I asked of him.

사무엘상 1:28.content: Therefore I have also given him to Yahweh. As long as he lives he is given to Yahweh.” He worshiped Yahweh there.
